title: Secure vault
subtitle: SSH passwords and private keys are encrypted on your device.

keychain.enabled: Keychain: auto-unlock enabled on this device
keychain.disabled: Keychain: not saved. Check remember when unlocking.

biometric.enabled: {{method}}: quick unlock enabled on this device

unlock.title: Vault
unlock.subtitle: Enter your master password to unlock.
unlock.rememberKeychain: Remember master password on this Mac (Keychain)
unlock.footnote: Terminal works without a vault too.

setup.title: Encrypted vault
setup.lead: Your SSH passwords and private keys are encrypted on your device. Optional — local terminal and host bookmarks work without it.
setup.rememberKeychain: Remember master password on this Mac (Keychain)

gate.devHint: From project root in terminal: pnpm dev

onboarding.title: How do you want to use termsh?
onboarding.subtitle: termsh keeps SSH passwords and private keys encrypted — never in plain text.
onboarding.chooseSubtitle: Choose how to get started. You can change this later in Settings.
onboarding.localLead: Encrypt passwords and keys on this device only. One master password unlocks everything.
onboarding.cloudLead: Back up hosts, snippets, and keys across devices. The server only sees encrypted data it cannot read.
onboarding.cloudRequiresVault: Requires a vault first — your master password never leaves this device.
onboarding.cloudNote: Sync account password only signs you in to the API; it cannot decrypt your data.
